title: pleopeltis griffithiana
intro: Pleopeltis griffithiana, also known as the Griffith's polypody, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pleopeltis griffithiana include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat wounds and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the leaves of this plant are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
